Title: A new approach to zip-lignin: 3,4-dihydroxybenzoate is compatible with lignification

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Shikimate pathway diversion linked to phenylpropanoid/lignin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: 3-dehydroshikimate-to-3,4-dihydroxybenzoate diversion branch upstream of monolignol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts 3-dehydroshikimate to 3,4-dihydroxybenzoate by dehydration, diverting carbon from the shikimate pathway away from phenylpropanoid/lignin precursor formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool; diverts flux toward DHBA and away from lignin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; acts as an engineered flux-diversion step
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: 3,4-dihydroxybenzoic acid (DHBA/DHB); lignin with DHB-derived zip-lignin features
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Phenolic acid; lignin-related aromatic polymer trait
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Introduces alkali-labile ester linkages into lignin, reduces biomass recalcitrance, and provides a potentially valorisable aromatic coproduct
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes strong accumulation of soluble and cell-wall-bound DHBA, reduces lignin content, increases H-lignin proportion, lowers lignin degree of polymerization, and improves saccharification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Xylem-specific expression of bacterial QsuB in hybrid poplar caused major accumulation of soluble DHBA in xylem and cell-wall-bound DHBA/glycosides. 2. QsuB expression reduced total lignin by up to 33% and increased H units to about 10% while reducing native p-hydroxybenzoate decoration. 3. 2D-NMR detected DHBA-derived benzodioxane structures, indicating DHB incorporation into the lignin backbone. 4. Biomass from transgenic lines released up to 40% more glucose and 33% more xylose after pretreatment and enzymatic hydrolysis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Agrobacterium-mediated transgenic expression; RT-qPCR of xylem transcripts; HPLC/UHPLC-HRMS metabolite profiling; alkaline/acid hydrolysis; Klason lignin assay; thioacidolysis; 2D-HSQC NMR; GPC; saccharification assays
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in xylem tissue of all transgenic lines; driven in lignifying xylem
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Glasshouse-grown trees for 5 months; ionic liquid pretreatment with cholinium lysinate before enzymatic saccharification
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Most transgenic lines had thinner stems, increased sylleptic branching, wood darkening/cracking on drying, mild collapsed xylem vessel phenotype, and increased dried wood density
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful engineering gene to create value-added zip-lignin feedstocks with lower recalcitrance and recoverable DHBA coproducts; suitable for woody biomass improvement when targeted to lignifying xylem
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper experimentally focuses on heterologous QsuB as the sole core gene. Other genes such as HCT, C3'H/Coumarate 3-hydroxylase, CSE, 4CL-like enzymes, O-methyltransferases, and p-hydroxybenzoyl-CoA monolignol transferase are discussed only as contextual or hypothetical contributors and were not functionally tested here.
